Data_Analysis

2026-06-07

Stay Probability vs Common/Rare

choice 1 –> state 2 is common ~69%

choice 1 –> state 3 is rare ~31%

choice 2 –> state 3 is common ~69%

choice 2 –> state 2 is rare ~32%

## # A tibble: 4 × 4
## # Groups:   choice1 [2]
##   choice1 state     n  prop
##     <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl>
## 1       1     2   977 0.694
## 2       1     3   431 0.306
## 3       2     2   301 0.317
## 4       2     3   648 0.683
# remove all the trials with NA (e.g. all the first trials where there is no lagging)

stay_analysis <- stay_df_clean %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(stay),
    !is.na(prev_reward),
    !is.na(prev_transition)
  )

stay_summary <- stay_analysis %>%
  mutate(
    prev_reward_label = ifelse(prev_reward == 1,
                               "Rewarded",
                               "Unrewarded")
  ) %>%
  group_by(participant_id,
           prev_reward_label,
           prev_transition) %>%
  summarise(
    stay_prob = mean(stay, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

stay_summary
## # A tibble: 48 × 4
##    participant_id prev_reward_label prev_transition stay_prob
##    <chr>          <chr>             <chr>               <dbl>
##  1 CCNP001339     Rewarded          Common              0.976
##  2 CCNP001339     Rewarded          Rare                0.867
##  3 CCNP001339     Unrewarded        Common              0.721
##  4 CCNP001339     Unrewarded        Rare                0.962
##  5 CCNP001445     Rewarded          Common              0.794
##  6 CCNP001445     Rewarded          Rare                0.667
##  7 CCNP001445     Unrewarded        Common              0.792
##  8 CCNP001445     Unrewarded        Rare                0.538
##  9 CCNP001604     Rewarded          Common              0.705
## 10 CCNP001604     Rewarded          Rare                0.811
## # ℹ 38 more rows
stay_summary <- stay_analysis %>%
  mutate(prev_reward_label = ifelse(prev_reward == 1, "Rewarded", "Unrewarded")) %>%
  group_by(participant_id, prev_reward_label, prev_transition) %>%
  summarise(
    stay_prob = mean(stay),
    se = sd(stay) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
#compute the stay probability & lag the reward & transition

stay_df <- stay_df %>%
  arrange(participant_id, trial) %>%
  group_by(participant_id) %>%
  mutate(
    # Stay: repeated same choice1 as previous trial
    stay = as.integer(choice1 == lag(choice1)),
    
    # Previous trial's reward and transition 
    prev_reward = lag(won),
    prev_transition = lag(transition)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Remove first trial of each subject 
  filter(!is.na(stay), !is.na(prev_reward), !is.na(prev_transition))

stay_summary <- stay_df %>%
  mutate(prev_reward_label = ifelse(prev_reward == 1, "Rewarded", "Unrewarded")) %>%
  group_by(participant_id, prev_reward_label, prev_transition) %>%
  summarise(stay_prob = mean(stay), .groups = "drop")

head(stay_summary)
## # A tibble: 6 × 4
##   participant_id prev_reward_label prev_transition stay_prob
##   <chr>          <chr>             <chr>               <dbl>
## 1 CCNP001339     Rewarded          Common              0.976
## 2 CCNP001339     Rewarded          Rare                0.867
## 3 CCNP001339     Unrewarded        Common              0.721
## 4 CCNP001339     Unrewarded        Rare                0.962
## 5 CCNP001445     Rewarded          Common              0.794
## 6 CCNP001445     Rewarded          Rare                0.667
stay_summary <- stay_df %>%
  mutate(prev_reward_label = ifelse(prev_reward == 1, "Rewarded", "Unrewarded")) %>%
  group_by(participant_id, prev_reward_label, prev_transition) %>%
  summarise(
    stay_prob = mean(stay),
    se = sd(stay) / sqrt(n()),  # standard error
    .groups = "drop"
  )

Plot for each subj

Check Transition Probability

# 1. Per rocket transition probability
rocket_transition <- stay_df_clean %>%
  group_by(choice1, state) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(choice1) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n),
         transition = case_when(
           choice1 == 1 & state == 2 ~ "Common",
           choice1 == 2 & state == 3 ~ "Common",
           TRUE ~ "Rare"
         ))

print(rocket_transition)
## # A tibble: 4 × 5
## # Groups:   choice1 [2]
##   choice1 state     n  prop transition
##     <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl> <chr>     
## 1       1     2   977 0.694 Common    
## 2       1     3   431 0.306 Rare      
## 3       2     2   301 0.317 Rare      
## 4       2     3   648 0.683 Common
# 2. Overall transition probability (collapsed across rockets)
overall_transition <- stay_df_clean %>%
  mutate(transition = case_when(
    choice1 == 1 & state == 2 ~ "Common",
    choice1 == 2 & state == 3 ~ "Common",
    TRUE ~ "Rare"
  )) %>%
  group_by(transition) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n))

print(overall_transition)
## # A tibble: 2 × 3
##   transition     n  prop
##   <chr>      <int> <dbl>
## 1 Common      1625 0.689
## 2 Rare         732 0.311
# 3. Block transition fraction (overall)
block_transition <- stay_df_clean %>%
  mutate(transition = case_when(
    choice1 == 1 & state == 2 ~ "Common",
    choice1 == 2 & state == 3 ~ "Common",
    TRUE ~ "Rare"
  )) %>%
  group_by(participant_id, transition) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(participant_id) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n))

print(block_transition)
## # A tibble: 24 × 4
## # Groups:   participant_id [12]
##    participant_id transition     n  prop
##    <chr>          <chr>      <int> <dbl>
##  1 CCNP001339     Common       144 0.72 
##  2 CCNP001339     Rare          56 0.28 
##  3 CCNP001445     Common       118 0.593
##  4 CCNP001445     Rare          81 0.407
##  5 CCNP001604     Common       137 0.692
##  6 CCNP001604     Rare          61 0.308
##  7 CCNP001613     Common       135 0.682
##  8 CCNP001613     Rare          63 0.318
##  9 CCNP001638     Common       136 0.694
## 10 CCNP001638     Rare          60 0.306
## # ℹ 14 more rows
# 4. Rocket transition fraction (by rocket, per subject)
rocket_transition_subj <- stay_df_clean %>%
  mutate(transition = case_when(
    choice1 == 1 & state == 2 ~ "Common",
    choice1 == 2 & state == 3 ~ "Common",
    TRUE ~ "Rare"
  )) %>%
  group_by(participant_id, choice1, transition) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(participant_id, choice1) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n))

print(rocket_transition_subj)
## # A tibble: 48 × 5
## # Groups:   participant_id, choice1 [24]

##    <chr>            <dbl> <chr>      <int> <dbl>
##  1 CCNP001339           1 Common        76 0.685
##  2 CCNP001339           1 Rare          35 0.315
##  3 CCNP001339           2 Common        68 0.764

##  5 CCNP001445           1 Common        89 0.601
##  6 CCNP001445           1 Rare          59 0.399
##  7 CCNP001445           2 Common        29 0.569

Data visualization for transition probability

# overall_transition with SE across subjects
overall_transition <- stay_df_clean %>%
  mutate(transition = case_when(
    choice1 == 1 & state == 2 ~ "Common",
    choice1 == 2 & state == 3 ~ "Common",
    TRUE ~ "Rare"
  )) %>%
  group_by(participant_id, transition) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(participant_id) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(transition) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_prop = mean(prop),
    se = sd(prop) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# rocket_transition with SE across subjects
rocket_transition <- stay_df_clean %>%
  mutate(transition = case_when(
    choice1 == 1 & state == 2 ~ "Common",
    choice1 == 2 & state == 3 ~ "Common",
    TRUE ~ "Rare"
  )) %>%
  group_by(participant_id, choice1, transition) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(participant_id, choice1) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(choice1, transition) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_prop = mean(prop),
    se = sd(prop) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Recompute block_transition with SE across trials
block_transition <- stay_df_clean %>%
  mutate(transition = case_when(
    choice1 == 1 & state == 2 ~ "Common",
    choice1 == 2 & state == 3 ~ "Common",
    TRUE ~ "Rare"
  )) %>%
  group_by(participant_id) %>%
  mutate(total_trials = n()) %>%
  group_by(participant_id, transition, total_trials) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    prop = n / total_trials,
    se = sqrt(prop * (1 - prop) / total_trials)  # binomial SE for proportions
  )

Log Regression - ind. level

Interpretation: - 7 subj with positive β1 and negative β3 (most subj are model based)

## # A tibble: 48 × 6
## # Groups:   participant_id [12]
##    participant_id term                      estimate std.error statistic p.value
##    <chr>          <chr>                        <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>
##  1 CCNP001339     (Intercept)                0.951       0.286    3.33   8.68e-4
##  2 CCNP001339     prev_reward                2.74        0.771    3.55   3.82e-4
##  3 CCNP001339     prev_transitionRare        2.27        1.06     2.14   3.22e-2
##  4 CCNP001339     prev_reward:prev_transit… -4.08        1.39    -2.95   3.22e-3
##  5 CCNP001445     (Intercept)                1.34        0.339    3.96   7.63e-5
##  6 CCNP001445     prev_reward                0.00730     0.460    0.0159 9.87e-1
##  7 CCNP001445     prev_transitionRare       -1.19        0.467   -2.54   1.11e-2
##  8 CCNP001445     prev_reward:prev_transit…  0.532       0.650    0.819  4.13e-1
##  9 CCNP001604     (Intercept)                1.37        0.289    4.74   2.18e-6
## 10 CCNP001604     prev_reward               -0.499       0.403   -1.24   2.16e-1
## # ℹ 38 more rows

##  3 CCNP001604             1.37     -0.499                0.189 
##  4 CCNP001613             0.445     2.34                 1.31

##  6 CCNP001665             0.804     0.617                0.582

##  8 CCNP001674             1.37      1.10                -0.672

## # ℹ 1 more variable: `prev_reward:prev_transitionRare` <dbl>
# Convert intercept to probability:

indiv_models_conv_prob <-
  indiv_models %>%
  filter(term == "(Intercept)") %>%
  select(participant_id, estimate) %>%
  mutate(baseline_stay_prob = 1 / (1 + exp(-estimate)))

indiv_models_conv_prob

## 10 CCNP001679       -1.91               0.129
## 11 CCNP001684        0.405              0.600
## 12 CCNP001689        0.312              0.577

Log Regression - mixed model effect

## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]
##  Family: binomial  ( logit )
## Formula: 
## stay ~ prev_reward * prev_transition + (1 + prev_reward * prev_transition |  
##     participant_id)
##    Data: stay_analysis
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##    2305.3    2385.7   -1138.6    2277.3      2302 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -4.8748  0.1684  0.3959  0.5723  2.3680 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups         Name                            Variance Std.Dev. Corr       
##  participant_id (Intercept)                     0.6747   0.8214              
##                 prev_reward                     1.6376   1.2797   -0.78      
##                 prev_transitionRare             0.6267   0.7916   -0.04  0.40
##                 prev_reward:prev_transitionRare 1.8173   1.3481   -0.23 -0.26
##       
##       
##       
##       
##  -0.92
## Number of obs: 2316, groups:  participant_id, 12
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                 Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                       0.5278     0.2509   2.103 0.035436 *  
## prev_reward                       1.4913     0.3971   3.755 0.000173 ***
## prev_transitionRare               0.4303     0.2820   1.526 0.127016    
## prev_reward:prev_transitionRare  -0.6841     0.4728  -1.447 0.147927    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) prv_rw prv_tR
## prev_reward -0.750              
## prv_trnstnR -0.123  0.365       
## prv_rwrd:_R -0.124 -0.315 -0.823
## # A tibble: 12 × 5
##    participant_id intercept_re prev_reward_re prev_transitionRare_re
##    <chr>                 <dbl>          <dbl>                  <dbl>
##  1 CCNP001339           0.426          0.723                  1.10  
##  2 CCNP001445           0.602         -1.45                  -1.11  
##  3 CCNP001604           0.758         -1.69                  -0.415 
##  4 CCNP001613          -0.0637         0.807                  0.653 
##  5 CCNP001638          -0.516         -0.424                  0.0563
##  6 CCNP001665           0.297         -0.793                 -0.0492
##  7 CCNP001670          -0.0804        -0.0855                 0.289 
##  8 CCNP001674           0.548         -0.297                 -0.530 
##  9 CCNP001678           0.469         -0.291                 -0.151 
## 10 CCNP001679          -2.25           2.93                  -0.433 
## 11 CCNP001684          -0.0296         0.848                  1.02  
## 12 CCNP001689          -0.218         -0.654                 -0.689 
## # ℹ 1 more variable: interaction_re <dbl>
